channel.title: Temple of Elemental Evil on Hi everyone. It's me, Tim.
channel.description: Recent content in Temple of Elemental Evil on Hi everyone. It's me, Tim.

item.title: Temple Of Elemental Evil Design Highlights
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about Temple of Elemental Evil design highlights.

This is a document I found in my archive. I was looking through it for something else, but I thought I’d share this because for all you people out there who are developing games, at some point you’re going to be asked to promote this game, maybe to press or maybe to fans, maybe at a conference or a convention, or maybe in a recorded interview or a playthrough. You should know what is important to talk about at such interviews and events, including what makes your game special or unique or why the press should be interested in it or why a player would want to play it I was asked by Atari to make this list, which they asked me. They said: “Could you make some design highlights”. This was because they were already planning for press events. This is dated May 13th 2003., there was a press event coming up in August, which is just a few months away. I wasn’t going to do that press event. That was going to be somebody else on the team. They had a family emergency. Someone else said they’d do it, but they didn’t have a passport that was valid, so I did it I have a video on this. It’s the Temple of Elemental Evil. Press event Took place in August 2003 in a castle south of London.

item.title: Questions To WotC About ToEE
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to share with you questions I sent to Wizards of the Coast about Temple of Elemental Evil.

This is a fun Friday, so this is not super serious, but it is important, I think, to dive into. Uh, let me tell you why I’m doing this. I’ve been looking for the test plan that I made for Tempo of Elemental Evil that I sent to Atari QA. I was planning to do a video on that. I’m still planning to do a video on that, but I can’t find the test plan And I know I wrote one.

item.title: Don't Forget Temple Of Elemental Evil 2003
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today, I want to ask you not to forget about Temple of Elemental Evil, my 2003 video game.

Now you’re probably going. I’m not going to forget about that. Why you? Why are you asking me not to forget about this?

Well, let me just say that I’ve talked about different reasons. I do this Channel and I think I found a new one.

I do not want this game to slip away into you — complete history — and no one ever mentions it or talks about it. Why am I bringing this up?

item.title: Temple Development Timeline
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today let’s do the Temple of Elemental Evil development timeline.

Now a lot of people liked my Fallout and Arcanum development timelines and they’re linked below. Although it’s interesting, a lot of people comment on them, comment on them, but they’re not well watched compared to my other videos. I don’t know if it’s because they’re long, but I just wanted to point out that a lot of you say you love them, but a lot of you don’t watch them, which is a perfect example of why sometimes the vocal people are not the majority and do not represent a majority opinion. Apparently there’s a lot of you out there who think these are boring, so let me give you a caveat ahead of time. I don’t know how long this is going to be. I made a lot of notes, but keep in mind that Temple was done in half the time of Fallout and Arcanum. Those two games took three and a half years. This game took a year and a half. Plus two months, so almost exactly 50% of the time. Also, for the LA, last 20% of this game’s development.

item.title: Thoughts On A Temple Remake
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about my thoughts on a temple remake, a remake of Temple of Elemental Evil, with things I would like to do that either I ran out of time to do or wish

I had done differently. Um, I’ve already talked about my thoughts on a Fallout remake — that was months ago and an Arcanum remake yesterday, so I thought I’d do a temple remake. I’ll link those other remakes below. I’m not going to do a remake video for vampire Bloodlines, though it’s not really mine to remake thoughts from the about. A remake on that should come from Leonard bski or Jason Anderson. So I’m just GNA complete my Trio of remake Thoughts with temple today. So let’s get started. I the first thing I’d do just the standard.

item.title: Temple Of Elemental Evil Press Event
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. And today I want to talk about the Temple of Elemental Evil press event.

I had a lot of fun talking a few days ago about going to the Fallout 3 launch party, so I thought I’d talk about this, because this is an actual crooked game. This happened in July of 2003. Atari was doing a press event, a combined preced event, for a lot of different games that they had, and they were doing it in London, which I thought was kind of interesting.

item.title: The Fun of The Temple of Elemental Evil
item.description:
Hi everyone, it’s me, Tim, so yesterday’s video about Temple development to evil was a little heavier.

I thought I’d follow it up with a description of how fun it was to make tempo of Elemental Evil so, kind of a Counterpoint to yesterday’s. The first thing I have to say is when I was approached about making a d game, I was ecstatic. I mean, 14 year old d playing Timmy was going. It was just super exciting, especially because I got to pick the module. You know, when I picked tempo of Unto evil — and just so people know that was the part of a grand plan — I was going to make Tempo valento evil. I was going to follow it up because I assumed it would do spectacularly with the D series modules, The Descent Into the Depths, the whole drow series, what it’s called — followed with the penultimate uh q6.

item.title: The Trials of The Temple of Elemental Evil
item.description:
Hi everyone, it’s me, Tim, so by now we’ve I’ve talked about troika’s games, Arcanum and bloodlines.

I’ve even talked about the secret DOD project, and I’ve talked about our demos for Lord of the Rings and for our post-apocalyptic game, Epic. I’ve not talked about Temple, so let’s talk about Temple. Temple it’s um troika’s worst scoring reviewed game. I think it’s the worst reviewed game of my career. I kind of want to explain how it became that and, for people who think I’m just gonna give excuses, this is an explanation and just so you know, everything I’m going to talk about is either the result of a bad decision I made — or because of a contract that I read, agreed to and signed, so I’m to blame for all this is. Get that out of the way, and now let me talk about what actually happened when we were making it, so towards the end, actually after Arcanum. So after Arcanum, we started working on Journey. To the Center of Arcanum and then that didn’t happen and so we started doing Lord of the Rings and that didn’t happen.
